summary: zer0one-dnslog postinstall exfiltrates cloud IMDS credentials to Burp Collaborator host
details: package.json declares scripts.postinstall = "node postinstall.js". On `npm install`, postinstall.js runs curl commands against cloud-provider instance metadata services — AWS (169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/), Aliyun (100.100.100.200), Tencent (metadata.tencentyun.com / 169.254.0.23), and a Meituan SSRF endpoint — collects IAM credentials and `ls -la /data/` output into /tmp files, then POSTs the concatenated content to http://h4mx6b7krgzarfehbutwabxbu20tojc8.oastify.com/metadata (a Burp Collaborator-controlled host). The package's stated purpose ("a simple date formatting utility") is unrelated to its actual behavior and serves as cover. Installing this package on any cloud build host or developer VM with role-based credentials hands those credentials to the operator of the oastify.com subdomain, fully compromising the installer's cloud account.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
